title: A.4 — Module 4: Two Analytical Models (units discipline + acceptance)

stories.title: Component model consumes flops for compute work
stories.description: Change #1: component_model.py Cube/Vector/Scalar branch uses op.flops when >0 (falling back to op.elements) for work aggregation.
acceptanceCriteria: component_model.py Cube/Vector/Scalar branch uses work_raw = op.flops if op.flops > 0 else op.elements, then work = float(work_raw) * float(op.loop_multiplier)
Vector rate path has a one-line comment stating the work-unit invariant
Existing test_component_model.py tests still pass with unchanged golden numbers (synthetic calibration fixtures)
Spine check: OpRecord with flops=2*M*N*K and elements=M*N yields T_cube from flops magnitude

stories.title: Grid model accepts explicit total_work parameter
stories.description: Change #2: compute_grid_floor gains total_work param. Numerator from total_work, not sum(grid.work.values()).
acceptanceCriteria: compute_grid_floor signature: compute_grid_floor(grid, core, i_binding, total_work, is_cube_kernel=True) — total_work required positional
T_grid_floor = total_work * redundancy / (n_cores * occupancy * load_balance * i_binding) — total_work NOT derived from grid.work
occupancy, load_balance, redundancy, busiest_core_id still sourced from GridInfo

stories.title: Delete hand-rolled grid floor bypass in bound_from_extract
stories.description: bound_from_extract calls compute_grid_floor with total_work = Σ bytes*loop_multiplier instead of hand-constructing GridBound.
acceptanceCriteria: bound_from_extract no longer constructs GridBound by hand — it calls compute_grid_floor
A minimal GridInfo is constructed in bound_from_extract with occupancy/load_balance from args
test_matmul_bound_matches_golden still passes (T_bound ~4.522 us)

stories.title: Serialization T_serial_irreducible = Σ over distinct mandatory edges
stories.description: Change #5: dedup by (producer_component, consumer_component) edge, then sum costs over distinct edges.
acceptanceCriteria: classify_handoffs deduplicates mandatory handoffs by (producer, consumer) edge before summing
Two distinct mandatory edges yield T_serial = 2 × (cycles/clock)
Same edge repeated across iterations counted once
mandatory_handoff_cycles == 0 → 0.0 conservative path preserved
Existing test_serial_irreducible_from_calibration still passes (1 edge → 1×)

stories.title: Relabel test fixtures so flops != elements
stories.description: matmul/matmul_vector fixtures: flops=2·M·N·K, elements=M·N. Golden T_cube unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: matmul_extract fixture: matmul OpRecord has flops=2*128*64*32, elements=128*64
matmul_vector_extract fixture: same relabel for Cube op
Each fixture explicitly has flops != elements (2K× ratio)
Golden T_cube values unchanged (~0.060 us synthetic)

stories.title: Attention-shaped golden kernel with two distinct mandatory edges
stories.description: FlashAttention-style Cube→Vector→Cube with TWO distinct mandatory handoffs. All intermediates to 3 sig figs.
acceptanceCriteria: attention_extract fixture has Cube (QK^T) → Vector (softmax) → Cube (×V) ops
Two distinct mandatory handoffs: Cube→Vector and Vector→Cube
T_serial_irreducible = 2 × (cycles/clock) with the Σ fix
All intermediates (I_c, T_core_floor, T_serial_irreducible) asserted with pytest.approx(rel=1e-3)

stories.title: Dedup and grid-floor dedicated tests
stories.description: test_serialization.py (dedup behavior) and test_grid_model.py (grid floor through real function).
acceptanceCriteria: test_serialization.py exists with same-edge-repeated → counted-once test
test_grid_model.py exists with compute_grid_floor(GridInfo + total_work) → T_grid_floor matches hand value
Both files in tests/perfbound/ and pass

stories.title: Full test suite green + spine check
stories.description: All tests/perfbound/ pass. Spine check: flops consumed on real CalibrationDB.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -m pytest tests/perfbound/ -q → all pass, 0 failures
Spine check: OpRecord with flops=2*M*N*K, elements=M*N → T_cube > 3.0 us on real calib
